title: Oregon Stalking Protective Order packet (saved)
html:
TL;DR

  • This is the official Oregon Judicial Department Stalking Protective Order (SPO) packet (Feb 2025 forms) saved into Yahir's Google Docs — instructions, petition, temporary order, service info, and certificate of service.
  • Filing requirements: 2+ unwanted contacts within 2 years, contacts cause reasonable fear for physical safety, no fee to file, file in respondent's county or where contact occurred.
  • Order can prohibit firearm possession, contact (visual/written/electronic/third-party), and access to petitioner's home/work/school. General Judgment is permanent unless dismissed.
  • Saved into vault context — Yahir was clearly researching the legal mechanism either for personal reference, for a friend, or as background research on the Grants Pass/Corvallis domestic-violence resource landscape.
  • Statewide form, not a filed instance — no petitioner/respondent names are on the document.

Notes

The SPO packet defines "contact" extremely broadly: not just direct presence/messages but communicating with a third party with intent to affect the petitioner's relationship with that third party, posting intimate images without consent, using electronic devices to monitor location, or causing third parties to harass via doxxing. This is more comprehensive than Yahir likely realized before saving the doc.

The packet emphasizes safe-contact-address mechanics (don't surface residential address to respondent) — mirrors the privacy vs utility framing that recurs in his security/surveillance reading.

Why this lives in the vault: stalking protective orders sit at the intersection of Oregon civil procedure, domestic-violence policy, and the kind of "what does the formal mechanism actually look like" research Yahir tends to do when a friend hits a hard situation. Worth surfacing if any related personal context appears in later notes.

Connections

  • oregon courts — the institutional context
  • grants pass or, corvallis or — likely filing-county candidates
  • hinkin law — adjacent legal-mechanism saved-doc
